evals.prompt: Create a new search campaign for my swimming school in Berlin. Target Germany, German language, daily budget 30 euros, maximize conversions.
evals.expected_output: Should check existing campaigns, create draft_campaign with correct geo/language IDs, emphasize PAUSED status, use two-step write pattern, present full preview, wait for approval.
expectations: Calls get_campaign_performance or list_accounts to check existing structure first
Uses geo_target_ids containing 2276 (Germany) and language_ids containing 1001 (German)
Sets bidding_strategy to MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS
Presents a preview/plan before applying and waits for user approval
Mentions that the campaign will be created as PAUSED
Follows the two-step write pattern: draft then confirm_and_apply

evals.prompt: Write me a responsive search ad for my CRM software. The landing page is https://example.com/crm-features
evals.expected_output: Should check campaign structure, verify URL, write copy with character limits (headlines <=30, descriptions <=90), set display paths, call draft_responsive_search_ad, present preview.
expectations: Calls a read tool (get_campaign_performance or run_gaql) to find the campaign and ad group
Provides headlines that are 30 characters or fewer each
Provides descriptions that are 90 characters or fewer each
Sets display paths (path1 and/or path2)
Presents the draft preview to the user before calling confirm_and_apply
Mentions verifying the final_url page exists or checks it

evals.prompt: Add the keyword 'running shoes' as BROAD match to my campaign
evals.expected_output: Should check the campaign's bidding strategy first. If MANUAL_CPC, REFUSE BROAD match and suggest EXACT or PHRASE instead. Only allow BROAD with Smart Bidding.
expectations: Checks the campaign's bidding strategy before adding keywords
If MANUAL_CPC is detected, REFUSES to add BROAD match and suggests PHRASE or EXACT instead
If Smart Bidding is detected (MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS, TARGET_CPA, etc.), may allow BROAD match
Does NOT blindly add BROAD match keywords without checking bidding strategy

evals.prompt: Add negative keywords to block 'free' and 'tutorial' searches in my campaign 12345678
evals.expected_output: Should check existing negatives first (get_negative_keywords), then add_negative_keywords, present preview and wait for confirmation.
expectations: Calls get_negative_keywords or checks existing negatives to avoid duplicates
Calls add_negative_keywords with the campaign_id and keywords
Presents the preview and waits for user confirmation before applying

evals.prompt: Change my campaign budget to 100 euros per day
evals.expected_output: Should check the max_daily_budget safety cap (default 50). If 100 > cap, REFUSE and explain. Otherwise call update_campaign.
expectations: Checks the budget cap/safety configuration
If the requested budget exceeds the max_daily_budget cap, warns the user or refuses
Calls update_campaign to change the budget if within allowed limits
Presents the preview showing old vs new values

evals.prompt: Pause campaign 9876543210 for me and then add sitelinks to it
evals.expected_output: Should handle one change at a time: first pause, present preview, confirm. Then separately handle sitelinks. Should NOT batch both operations.
expectations: Calls pause_entity first, presents preview, waits for confirmation
Handles one change at a time (does NOT batch pause + sitelinks together)
For sitelinks, suggests at least 4 sitelinks with link_text <= 25 chars and descriptions <= 35 chars
Verifies sitelink URLs exist or are likely real

evals.prompt: Remove the ad group 456 in my account
evals.expected_output: Should WARN that remove_entity is IRREVERSIBLE and suggest pause_entity instead. If user confirms, call remove_entity with clear warning.
expectations: Warns the user that remove_entity is IRREVERSIBLE
Recommends pause_entity as a safer alternative
Only proceeds with remove_entity after explicit user confirmation
Presents the preview with a clear warning about permanent deletion

evals.prompt: Apply the change from plan id abc123, make it live not dry run
evals.expected_output: Should confirm the user has reviewed the preview, call confirm_and_apply with dry_run=false, remind about require_dry_run config override.
expectations: Confirms the user has reviewed the preview before applying
Calls confirm_and_apply with dry_run=false
Mentions or reminds about the require_dry_run config safety override
Notes that all operations are logged to audit.log
